feedback.text: I'm an American, but I'd like to propose you look at requiring operating system companies to allow sideloading of applications. Google is talking about restricting sideloading under the guise of it being "required" by different online privacy regulations, but they really just want an excuse to prevent people from sideloading YouTube apps that don't have ads. The truth is there are plenty of very useful apps that are not in the play store that can be side loaded and the use of sideloading is mostly legitimate. I would love to contact my own government, but they don't do anything useful so I'm hoping if the EU puts that requirement in place, it could push Google to allow it for other areas as well. There are many others like me who are very frustrated by this proposal by Google and we hope you can enact some sort of protection for consumers in this case. Thank you.
